title: ceropegia mariae
intro: Ceropegia mariae, commonly known as the "rope plant" or "voodoo lily," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant is traditionally used in African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Ceropegia mariae include the ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a treatment for snake bites. Herbal preparations of Ceropegia mariae can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and are often combined with other plants for enhanced medicinal effects.
